best_edges.evidence: pullback_long is the largest PnL contributor: 31 trades, $2411.8 PnL, 252.1 PnL/exposure hour, 100% win rate, no slow >=12h trades. Tail is meaningful at -403.2 worst MAE, but broad pruning risks damaging the core edge.

charm_wall_breakaway_long has only 5 trades but $985.6 PnL, 197.1 average PnL, 234.4 PnL/exposure hour, median MFE 146.2, no slow trades, and worst MAE -346.8. Prior geometry follow-through was flat, while exit/TP compression hurt similar convexity in squeeze tests.

breakout_long_1dte contributes $1300.9 over 20 VALID_1DTE_ONLY trades with no slow trades. Prior direct tightening of 1DTE structure/VWAP/TP gates reduced net profit by $317-$558 and worsened MAE, so the existing edge should not be tightened bluntly.

Extended-VWAP winners contribute $715.7 across 19 trades with median MAE -15.3 and median hold 2.27 minutes. The worst tail is also extended, so risk work must isolate delayed progress rather than block extension itself.

best_edges.edge: Squeeze continuation with clean option context
best_edges.evidence: squeeze_long has 10 trades, $721 PnL, worst MAE -208.25, no slow trades, and strong option-map context. However, the latest squeeze fast-realization compression hurt score and worsened worst MAE, so this edge should be protected from lower-target compression.

broken_edges.evidence: The worst-MAE fingerprint shows 24 bad rows are covered by the extended-VWAP rejection near-miss family, but single-threshold candidates retain only 46%-86% PnL and often still leave worst MAE at -513.4. ADX slope >=0 would block $2846.8 PnL and 24 protected good trades.
broken_edges.problem: Static broad filters are too correlated with profitable fast winners.

broken_edges.evidence: Two prior 1DTE quality-gate experiments reduced score by -355.7 to -594.3, cut net profit by $317.1-$558.5, and worsened worst MAE to -603.75.
broken_edges.problem: The current 1DTE breakout edge is harmed by stricter structure/VWAP/TP compression.

broken_edges.evidence: Prior pullback expansion raised dynamic TP and ES-VWAP max distance, causing drawdown to rise from 0.004 to 0.020, worst MAE to worsen from -527 to -2026.5, and one slow >=12h trade.
broken_edges.problem: Loosening pullback exposure creates large hidden tail risk in a no-stop strategy.

broken_edges.action: deprioritize stale no-0DTE compression as primary lever
broken_edges.evidence: Latest stale/no-0DTE breakout compression was near-flat but slightly negative: score -11.9, net profit -$12.5, exposure +2.46 hours, no worst-MAE improvement. STALE_0DTE total sample is only 5 trades and $145.8 PnL.
broken_edges.problem: Small sample and weak objective impact; useful only as a low-priority holdout.

broken_edges.action: freeze dead enabled/disabled profiles unless used for explicit discovery
broken_edges.evidence: breakout_long, wall_reclaim_long, wall_breakout_retest_long, late_day_pin_fade, negative_gamma_momentum_long, and trend_day_pullback_long produced zero trades in the latest run.
broken_edges.problem: No current evidence of live edge; optimizing thresholds without activation evidence is noise.

market_regime_read: Short, high-win-rate, take-profit-only sample over about 12.3 calendar days. Realized losses and slow >=12h trades are absent, but hidden MAE is material: average MAE about -98.6 and worst MAE -513.4. The risk-adjusted problem is not classic stop-loss tuning; it is reducing delayed-to-profit heat while preserving fast winners. The current leader remains run_029 with $7149.6 net profit and -513.4 worst MAE; latest run_035 is slightly lower PnL at $7057.0 with similar worst MAE. The main tail is charm_pin_pullback_long extended/charm-pin state, while pullback_long and 1DTE remain important PnL engines that should not be broadly pruned.

new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: May overfit a tiny set of deep-MAE recoveries or block clean high-extension winners unless fast-winner retention is explicitly validated.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Charm Pin Early-Progress Tail Classifier
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: Intratrade path features including first +1.5/+2.0 point time, first 15m MFE/MAE, MFE-before-MAE, TP-before-MAE, and profile/extension context.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: charm_pin_pullback_long entries with high ES-VWAP extension or recent-high pullback that fail to make early positive progress after entry.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Reduce charm_pin_pullback_long worst MAE toward <= -403 while retaining >=90% charm-pin PnL, >=95% protected fast extended-VWAP winners, total net profit >=98% of leader, and slow_12h remains 0.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: 1DTE state split rather than breakout tightening
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Router overlap may cannibalize profitable breakout_long_1dte trades or the companion profile may be unreachable.
new_strategy_ideas.name: 1DTE Pullback Companion Profile
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: VALID_1DTE_ONLY option state, ES pullback/reclaim structure, existing breakout_long_1dte trade attribution, and router split diagnostics.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: 0DTE unavailable, 1DTE map valid, and ES state resembles pullback/reclaim rather than breakout continuation.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Combined 1DTE PnL >= current $1300.9, 1DTE average MAE improves versus -123.7, total net profit >=98% of leader, and total score flat or better.

new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Dormant profile may be unreachable or may cannibalize clean charm-pin/charm-wall winners rather than isolate tails.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Charm Wall Pin-Avoid Dormant Activation Probe
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: Router attribution by profile, charm wall/pin proximity, protected fast-winner list, profile standalone PnL and MAE.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: VALID_0DTE high charm-wall or pin-risk states currently routed into charm_pin_pullback_long or charm_wall_breakaway_long.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Activated dormant profile has positive standalone PnL or reduces combined charm-pin/charm-wall worst MAE while total net profit remains >=98% of leader and protected fast-winner retention remains >=95%.

new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Static config proxies may block profitable weak-structure pullbacks; path-aware features may be required.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Pullback Deep-Heat Companion Classifier
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: pullback_long entries with early path progress, ES structure distances, VWAP acceleration, setup quality, and delayed recovery behavior.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: VALID_0DTE pullback_long entries with weak structure, poor early MFE, or adverse first move.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Retain >=90% of pullback_long PnL while improving pullback worst MAE from -403.2 and keeping total PnL/exposure hour flat or better.

new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Sample is only one trade; loosening global call-volume or DEX gates can dilute the portfolio edge.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Call Wall Acceptance Sample Builder
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: call_wall_acceptance_long standalone trade count, acceptance duration, call-volume increase, forward DEX ratio, net DEX volume, and MAE.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: VALID_0DTE call-wall acceptance with strong ADX/RSI and nearby upside call-volume support.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: At least 3 total call_wall_acceptance_long trades, positive standalone PnL, profile PnL/exposure >=250, worst MAE <= -150, and total net profit >=98% of leader.

next_experiments.config_bias: Nudge the already-enabled extended-VWAP rejection near-miss boundaries one family at a time. Prioritize the smallest boundary that catches the -513.4 charm-pin row without broadly expanding to high-RSI or zero-pullback 1DTE/charm-wall rows. Avoid global ADX slope, setup floor, or ES-VWAP max-distance pruning.
next_experiments.goal: Reduce worst MAE by catching the current charm-pin tail while preserving extended-VWAP fast winners.
next_experiments.hypothesis: The worst tail missed an enabled veto by a narrow ES-VWAP distance boundary, so a surgical threshold repair can improve tail MAE with less PnL loss than broad filters.
next_experiments.name: Charm Pin Extended-VWAP Near-Miss Repair
next_experiments.primary_metric: Worst MAE, charm_pin_pullback_long PnL retention >=90%, protected extended-VWAP winner retention >=95%, total net profit >=98% of run_029.
next_experiments.risk: The same veto family covers many profitable extended trades; over-widening can cut fast PnL and lower score.

next_experiments.config_bias: Use profile-aware charm-pin tail-risk or broken-structure guard refinement around high pullback-from-high, high rolling-high distance, weak/negative VWAP acceleration, low ADX/RSI, and profile match required. Keep changes narrow and avoid setup-quality floors.
next_experiments.goal: Reduce charm_pin_pullback_long hidden heat without suppressing its fast pullback edge.
next_experiments.hypothesis: The -513.4 tail combines high ES-VWAP extension, pullback-from-high 9.25, rolling-high distance 14.25, VWAP acceleration -0.0179, ADX 17.95, RSI 42.42, and 80-minute delayed TP; a profile-specific tail box is more appropriate than a global filter.
next_experiments.name: Charm Pin Tail Box Refinement
next_experiments.primary_metric: charm_pin_pullback_long worst MAE, profile PnL retention, total score, fast_under_15m count retention.
next_experiments.risk: The bad row still closed profitably; a static box may remove profitable delayed recoveries unless bounded tightly.

next_experiments.config_bias: Test dormant charm_wall_pin_avoid_long as an attribution split through the valid-0DTE router only if the optimizer can make one full router replacement plus minimal same-profile activation/tuning. Do not alter existing charm_pin_pullback_long or charm_wall_breakaway_long thresholds in the same test.
next_experiments.goal: Discover whether an existing pin-avoid playbook can absorb tail-heavy charm/pin states without bluntly pruning current profiles.
next_experiments.hypothesis: The current worst tail is a charm-pin/high-extension state, while protected fast extension winners show this should be split rather than globally blocked.
next_experiments.name: Dormant Charm-Wall Pin-Avoid Split Probe
next_experiments.primary_metric: Activated profile standalone PnL, combined charm-pin/charm-wall/pin-avoid worst MAE, total net profit >=98% of run_029, protected fast-winner retention >=95%.
next_experiments.risk: Router order can cannibalize clean charm-pin winners or the dormant profile may not fire.

next_experiments.config_bias: Keep breakout_long_1dte thresholds stable. If legal, test a companion pullback_long_1dte router split; otherwise freeze 1DTE and do not repeat direct 1DTE tightening.
next_experiments.goal: Improve 1DTE MAE through segmentation rather than stricter breakout filters.
next_experiments.hypothesis: 1DTE contributes $1300.9 but average MAE is -123.7; prior direct quality tightening hurt, suggesting a companion-state split is the only credible risk-reducer path.
next_experiments.name: 1DTE Companion Pullback Split
next_experiments.primary_metric: Combined 1DTE PnL, 1DTE average/worst MAE, total risk-adjusted score, breakout_long_1dte cannibalization.
next_experiments.risk: Companion profile may reduce high-quality breakout fallback trades or be unreachable.

next_experiments.config_bias: Run only a narrow stale/no-0DTE holdout if it is isolated to no-0DTE profiles and does not affect VALID_0DTE. Focus on exposure efficiency, not aggressive removal; do not sacrifice the single EXPIRED_0DTE high-efficiency trade.
next_experiments.goal: Confirm whether stale fallback exposure can be reduced without hurting the main edge.
next_experiments.hypothesis: STALE_0DTE contributes only $145.8 over 5 trades with low PnL/exposure, so small efficiency gains are possible, but prior compression was nearly flat and should not be primary.
next_experiments.name: Stale No-0DTE Efficiency Holdout
next_experiments.primary_metric: Fallback PnL/exposure hour, fallback worst MAE, unchanged VALID_0DTE PnL/trade count, total net profit >=98% of run_029.
next_experiments.risk: Very small sample; stricter fallback gates may remove insurance without improving portfolio MAE.

next_experiments.config_bias: Freeze exits and TP compression. If tested, use only small admission/sample-builder probes around supported wall geometry; preserve require-breakout behavior.
next_experiments.goal: Protect and possibly grow the high-convexity charm-wall edge while monitoring tail MAE.
next_experiments.hypothesis: charm_wall_breakaway_long has strong PnL/exposure and manageable worst MAE, but sample size is only 5 trades; admission work may add evidence, while exit compression risks underpaying convex winners.
next_experiments.name: Charm Wall Supported Admission Holdout
next_experiments.primary_metric: charm_wall_breakaway_long PnL/exposure >=225, trade count, total net profit >=98% of leader, total worst MAE no worse than -513.4.
next_experiments.risk: Small-sample geometry loosening can add weaker wall-chase entries or cannibalize existing winners.

research_notes: The strongest instruction for the optimizer is to avoid broad single-threshold pruning. The bad-MAE rows are real, but most static separators either retain too little PnL or still leave the worst MAE. Extended-VWAP is both the tail source and a fast-winner source; risk reduction should use narrow veto-boundary repair, profile-specific tail boxes, or router/profile splits. The current base run_029 remains the correct anchor; run_035 did not improve enough to justify changing the research direction.
risk_controls: Require total net profit >=98% of run_029 unless worst MAE improves materially beyond -403.
Require protected fast-winner retention >=95% for any extended-VWAP or charm-pin entry filter.
Do not repeat direct breakout_long_1dte quality tightening; only test companion split or freeze.
Do not loosen pullback_long ES-VWAP max distance or raise pullback TP after prior -2026.5 MAE failure.
Keep slow_12h_trades at 0 for all promoted experiments.
Treat STALE_0DTE and EXPIRED_0DTE results as small-sample diagnostics, not core optimization targets.
Do not activate multiple dormant profiles in one experiment; use exactly one router activation split at a time.
